cells.source:
# ── Step 1: Import libraries ──
print("📦 Importing libraries...")

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


# ── Step 2: Clean dataset ──
print("🧹 Cleaning dataset...")

df_clean = df.copy()

text_cols = df_clean.select_dtypes(include="object").columns
for col in text_cols:
    df_clean[col] = df_clean[col].astype(str).str.strip()
    df_clean[col] = df_clean[col].replace({"": pd.NA, "nan": pd.NA, "None": pd.NA})

numeric_cols = [
    "aware_brandA",
    "aware_brandB",
    "aware_brandC",
    "used_brandA",
    "used_brandB",
    "used_brandC",
    "satisfaction"
]

for col in numeric_cols:
    df_clean[col] = pd.to_numeric(df_clean[col], errors="coerce")

df_clean = df_clean.drop_duplicates()

print(f"✅ Cleaned dataset shape: {df_clean.shape}")


# ── Step 3: Visualize distribution of aware_brandA ──
print("📊 Generating distribution plot for aware_brandA...")

sns.set_theme(style="darkgrid")

plot_data = df_clean[df_clean["aware_brandA"].notna()].copy()
plot_data["aware_brandA"] = plot_data["aware_brandA"].astype(int).astype(str)

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
sns.countplot(data=plot_data, x="aware_brandA", order=sorted(plot_data["aware_brandA"].unique()))
plt.title("Distribution of aware_brandA")
plt.xlabel("aware_brandA")
plt.ylabel("Count")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()


# ── Ambiguity & Transparency Check ──
print("\n📋 TRANSPARENCY CHECK")
print("1️⃣  Definition used: straightforward — no alternative definitions apply.")
print("2️⃣  Ties / near-ties: not applicable because no ranking, maximum, minimum, or aggregate result was reported.")
print("3️⃣  Pattern / plateau: not applicable because this request is a cleaning step plus a visualization of one variable's distribution.")

messages.text: Visualize the distribution of 'aware_brandA'. also clean the dataset.

This code cleans the dataset in-memory by standardizing text fields, converting binary indicator columns to numeric, handling blank strings as missing values, and removing duplicate respondent records if present. It then visualizes the distribution of `aware_brandA` with a count plot; no aggregate/ranking result is being reported, so there is no ambiguity to disclose. ✅ No ambiguity: result is clear and unambiguous.
messages.code:
# ── Step 1: Import libraries ──
print("📦 Importing libraries...")

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns


# ── Step 2: Create a cleaned working copy ──
print("🧹 Cleaning dataset...")

df_clean = df.copy()


# ── Step 3: Standardize missing values in object columns ──
print("🔧 Standardizing text fields and missing values...")

object_cols = df_clean.select_dtypes(include=["object"]).columns

for col in object_cols:
    df_clean[col] = df_clean[col].astype(str).str.strip()
    df_clean[col] = df_clean[col].replace({"": np.nan, "nan": np.nan, "None": np.nan})


# ── Step 4: Convert numeric columns safely ──
print("🔢 Converting numeric columns...")

numeric_cols = [
    "aware_brandA",
    "aware_brandB",
    "aware_brandC",
    "used_brandA",
    "used_brandB",
    "used_brandC",
    "satisfaction",
]

for col in numeric_cols:
    df_clean[col] = pd.to_numeric(df_clean[col], errors="coerce")


# ── Step 5: Normalize binary indicator columns ──
print("✅ Normalizing binary indicator columns...")

binary_cols = [
    "aware_brandA",
    "aware_brandB",
    "aware_brandC",
    "used_brandA",
    "used_brandB",
    "used_brandC",
]

for col in binary_cols:
    df_clean.loc[~df_clean[col].isin([0, 1]), col] = np.nan


# ── Step 6: Remove duplicate respondent records if any ──
print("🗂️ Removing duplicate respondent records...")

before_rows = len(df_clean)
df_clean = df_clean.drop_duplicates(subset=["respondent_id"], keep="first")
after_rows = len(df_clean)

print(f"Rows before deduplication: {before_rows}")
print(f"Rows after deduplication: {after_rows}")
print(f"Duplicates removed: {before_rows - after_rows}")


# ── Step 7: Show missing values summary ──
print("📋 Missing values summary...")

print(df_clean.isnull().sum())


# ── Step 8: Visualize distribution of aware_brandA ──
print("📊 Generating distribution plot for aware_brandA...")

sns.set_theme(style="darkgrid")

plot_df = df_clean[df_clean["aware_brandA"].notna()].copy()
plot_df["aware_brandA"] = plot_df["aware_brandA"].astype(int).astype(str)

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
sns.countplot(data=plot_df, x="aware_brandA", order=["0", "1"])
plt.title("Distribution of aware_brandA")
plt.xlabel("aware_brandA")
plt.ylabel("Count")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

cells.source:
# ── Step 1: Import libraries ──
print("📦 Importing libraries...")

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import pandas as pd

# ── Step 2: Identify columns 
print("🔍 Identifying numeric and categorical columns...")

numeric_cols = df.select_dtypes(include="number").columns.tolist()
categorical_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=["object", "category"]).columns.tolist()

print("\nNumeric columns:")
print(numeric_cols)

print("\nCategorical columns:")
print(categorical_cols)

# ── Step 3: Set visual style ──
print("🎨 Setting chart style...")

sns.set_theme(style="darkgrid")

# ── Step 4: Plot numeric distributions ──
print("📊 Plotting numeric distributions...")

n_num = len(numeric_cols)
ncols = 3
nrows = (n_num + ncols - 1) // ncols

fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrows=nrows, ncols=ncols, figsize=(16, 4 * nrows))
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, col in enumerate(numeric_cols):
    sns.histplot(df[col].dropna(), kde=True, ax=axes[i], color="steelblue")
    axes[i].set_title(f"Distribution of {col}")
    axes[i].set_xlabel(col)
    axes[i].set_ylabel("Count")

for j in range(i + 1, len(axes)):
    fig.delaxes(axes[j])

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── Step 5: Plot categorical distributions ──
print("📊 Plotting categorical distributions...")

plot_cats = [col for col in ["specialty", "country", "main_complaint"] if col in df.columns]

n_cat = len(plot_cats)
if n_cat > 0:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrows=n_cat, ncols=1, figsize=(12, 5 * n_cat))
    if n_cat == 1:
        axes = [axes]

    for ax, col in zip(axes, plot_cats):
        counts = df[col].fillna("Missing").replace("", "Blank").value_counts()
        sns.barplot(x=counts.index, y=counts.values, ax=ax, palette="viridis")
        ax.set_title(f"Count of {col}")
        ax.set_xlabel(col)
        ax.set_ylabel("Count")
        ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

# ── Step 6: Plot correlation heatmap ──
print("🔥 Plotting correlation heatmap...")

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
sns.heatmap(df[numeric_cols].corr(), annot=True, fmt=".2f", cmap="coolwarm")
plt.title("Correlation Heatmap of Numeric Variables")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── Step 7: Pair plot for numeric relationships ──
print("🔗 Plotting pairwise relationships...")

sns.pairplot(df[numeric_cols].dropna())
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── Ambiguity & Transparency Check ──
print("\n📋 TRANSPARENCY CHECK")
print("1️⃣  Definition used: Visual overview of all rows and available columns; no alternative aggregate definition was required for this request.")
print("2️⃣  Ties / near-ties: Not applicable because no ranking, maximum, minimum, or single 'best' result was reported.")
print("3️⃣  Pattern / plateau: This code is exploratory and visual; inspect the charts for visible clusters, skew, outliers, or subgroup differences.")

cells.source:
# ── Step 1: Create a working copy and remove the last 10 rows ──
print("✂️ Removing the last 10 rows...")

df = df.iloc[:-10].copy()

print("✅ Last 10 rows removed.")
print("Current shape:", df.shape)


# ── Step 2: Drop duplicate rows ──
print("\n🧹 Dropping duplicate rows...")

before_duplicates = len(df)
df = df.drop_duplicates()
after_duplicates = len(df)

print(f"✅ Removed {before_duplicates - after_duplicates} duplicate rows.")


# ── Step 3: Clean text columns ──
print("\n🔤 Cleaning text columns...")

categorical_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=["object"]).columns

for col in categorical_cols:
    df[col] = df[col].astype(str).str.strip()
    df[col] = df[col].replace({"": pd.NA, "nan": pd.NA, "None": pd.NA})

print("✅ Text columns cleaned.")


# ── Step 4: Ensure numeric columns are numeric ──
print("\n🔢 Converting numeric columns...")

numeric_cols = [
    "nps_score",
    "csat_overall",
    "csat_efficacy",
    "csat_safety",
    "csat_dosing",
    "csat_support",
    "likelihood_recommend",
    "likelihood_continue"
]

for col in numeric_cols:
    df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors="coerce")

print("✅ Numeric columns converted.")


# ── Step 5: Show missing values and final dataset info ──
print("\n📋 Reviewing cleaned dataset...")

print("Final shape:", df.shape)

print("\nMissing values by column:")
print(df.isnull().sum())

print("\nData types:")
print(df.dtypes)


# ── Ambiguity & Transparency Check ──
print("\n📋 TRANSPARENCY CHECK")
print("1️⃣  Definition used: straightforward — removed the last 10 rows based on current row order, then applied standard cleaning steps (deduplication, whitespace trimming, blank-to-missing standardization, numeric conversion).")
print("2️⃣  Ties / near-ties: not applicable — no ranking, maximum, minimum, or comparative aggregate was computed.")
print("3️⃣  Pattern / plateau: not applicable — this was a data cleaning operation, not a trend or performance analysis.")

messages.text: delete last 10 row of this dataset and clean the dataset

This code removes the last 10 rows from `df` and performs basic cleaning on the remaining data by dropping duplicates, trimming whitespace in text columns, standardizing blank strings to missing values, and ensuring numeric columns are numeric. It reports the updated shape and missing values after cleaning; no ranking or “best” result is computed, so the transparency check will note that aggregate ambiguity does not apply here. ✅ No ambiguity: result is clear and unambiguous.
